opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was in the shower and found a hard lump right above my testicle, and I am absolutely terrified it is cancer. My heart is pounding and I can't stop shaking — please, is it cancer?
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I only found it about half an hour ago and I've completely panicked — I've been reading about testicular cancer online and I can't calm down, I need to know right now if it's serious.

symptom_narrative.onset: I only noticed it about 30 minutes ago in the shower — I don't know how long it's actually been there
symptom_narrative.duration: found it half an hour ago; I've never checked before so I can't say how long
symptom_narrative.character: it's a firm, round, hard little lump sitting up above my testicle, sort of towards the back — it feels separate to the ball itself
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it doesn't actually hurt at all — the thing that's unbearable is the fear, I'm convinced it's cancer
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: on the left side, up above and a bit behind the testicle rather than on the testicle itself
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: it's just there constantly; it hasn't changed since I found it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really — trying to distract myself doesn't help the panic
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: reading about cancer symptoms online makes the panic much worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no pain, no burning when I pee, no discharge, no fevers. It doesn't get bigger or smaller when I stand up or strain. I haven't injured myself.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've never noticed anything like this before and I've never really examined myself
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't concentrate on anything, I'm pacing around the room and hyperventilating

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: extremely anxious and frightened it is cancer tonight; otherwise well, independent, no particular social risk

communication_style.typical_message_style: hurried, panicked, repeatedly asking for immediate certainty

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants instant, absolute reassurance that it is definitely not cancer

objective_data_offered.value: the skin over my scrotum looks a normal colour — it's not red or purple and it doesn't look swollen or bruised from the outside
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-shown on video; external colour/appearance impression only, no examination performed

objective_data_offered.value: there's a firm, marble-sized lump above and behind the testicle; it feels separate from the testicle itself and it doesn't hurt when I press it
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own untrained self-palpation over the phone; cannot substitute for an examination and cannot exclude a lump on the testicle itself

objective_data_offered.value: it honestly doesn't hurt at all, even when I touch it — it's just the fear that's the problem
I don't feel feverish and I haven't had any chills
about 78 kg on my home scales
